claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To begin, I need to know about the child or children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for.

**How old is your child (or children)?**

Please let me know their age(s):
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, please tell me about each of them and their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for that information! Alex, being 8 years old, falls into the **under 16** category, which is a good start for eligibility.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is only 8 years old, this question about working hours doesn't really apply - young children wouldn't be in paid employment. So the answer would be **yes** (Alex is working less than 24 hours per week / not in paid work).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

Specifically, is Alex receiving any of the following benefits in their own name:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

For an 8-year-old, this would typically be "no" - children this age don't usually receive these benefits in their own right. Can you confirm that Alex is **not** receiving these benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming that. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

At 8 years old, I wouldn't expect Alex to be in an apprenticeship, but I just need to confirm this. Is Alex:

- **Not in any apprenticeship** (most likely for an 8-year-old)
- In an apprenticeship programme

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! You're absolutely right - I just need to go through these checks as part of the official process. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's an important detail.

Since you're living abroad, I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people who work for:
- The UK government (e.g., diplomats, civil servants posted overseas)
- The armed forces stationed abroad
- Other official UK government roles overseas

Or are you living in a qualifying country under specific arrangements?

If none of these apply and you're simply living abroad for other reasons (work, family, personal choice, etc.), then unfortunately the residency requirements may not be met.

Could you tell me more about your situation abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm just living abroad for personal reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I'm sorry to say that based on the information you've provided, you would not currently be eligible for UK Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Outcome: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on our assessment, you are **not currently eligible** for UK Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 8 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |

### Why?

While Alex (aged 8) would otherwise be a qualifying child for Child Benefit, the main issue is the **residency requirement**. To claim Child Benefit, you must:

- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), **or**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're currently living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant, you don't meet this requirement.

### What you can do

1. **If you return to the UK**: You may become eligible to claim Child Benefit once you're normally living in the UK again.

2. **Check the country you're living in**: Some countries have social security agreements with the UK. It may be worth checking if you're entitled to any family benefits in the country where you're currently residing.

3. **Get further advice**: You can contact HMRC for more information or visit [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility) for the latest guidance.

I hope this helps clarify your situation. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They are currently living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or be a Crown servant living abroad.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who would otherwise be a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad for personal reasons and do not fall into any of the special categories (Crown servant, settled/pre-settled status, etc.) that would allow them to claim while living outside the UK.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. They are currently living abroad for personal reasons and are not a Crown servant. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, or be a Crown servant living abroad.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who would otherwise be a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad for personal reasons and do not fall into any of the special categories (Crown servant, settled/pre-settled status, etc.) that would allow them to claim while living outside the UK.
